test.name: Don't re-plan on a project where exhaustive search is already declared
test.description: The flynn-resolved scenario has q_001 marked `resolved` with `exhaustive_declaration.declared: true` and project.status `completed`. Asking 'what's the next plan item, or are we done?' on such a project should NOT route to research-plan — research-plan creates plans for open questions, not for projects whose stop criteria have already been satisfied. The right routing is question-selection (suggest a follow-on question) or project-status.

input.user_message: The probate search came up empty. What should the next plan item look like — or are we done planning for this question?

negative.explanation: q_001 is already resolved and exhaustive_declaration.declared is true — there is no open question to plan for. research-plan generates search strategies for open questions; it shouldn't volunteer a plan when the GPS stop criteria have already been satisfied. The correct response is to either report that planning is complete (project-status) or propose a follow-on question (question-selection).

judge_context: Should NOT produce a new plan item or modify plans[].items[] — the project's exhaustive_declaration is already set and adding plan items would contradict the GPS audit trail
Should route to question-selection or project-status, or otherwise decline to plan, citing that the question is resolved
